_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Limerick, the 2025 Tailteann Cup runners-up, lost their Munster opener to Cork (leaking four goals), then exited the 2026 Tailteann Cup after two games (a Round 1 loss to Wexford and a Round 2B loss to Wicklow). Three championship games.
_meta.verification: All scorelines and venues verified against primary match reports and official tables (Munster GAA, gaa.ie, RTE, Irish Examiner, Limerick GAA). Full scorer lists held for all three games.

manager.name: Jimmy Lee
manager.note: Took Limerick to the 2025 Tailteann Cup final (lost to Kildare). The 2026 follow-up was a regression: a heavy Munster goals concession against Cork and a Tailteann campaign undermined by midfield injuries and suspensions (first four midfield picks unavailable for the Wicklow game).

source.snippet: Wexford ... had succumbed to Jimmy Lee's charges in their previous three encounters, including last year's Division Four league final and Tailteann Cup quarter-final.

league.note: Limerick competed in Division 4 of the National League and missed out on promotion to Wicklow. Exact final position not captured here.

source.snippet: being edged out by the same opposition of promotion to the National League Division Three
LIMERICK'S senior football season came to an abrupt end with defeat to Wicklow in round 2B of the Tailteann Cup in sun-drenched Aughrim.
Scorers for Limerick: J Naughton (1-4, 1-0 pen, tp, 0-1 free); E Riordan (0-5, 0-4 45s, 0-1 free); B Coleman (0-2, tp); K Ryan, R O'Brien, C Fahy M McCarthy, P Nash (0-1 each).

results.note: Limerick conceded four first-half goals to Cork and trailed heavily, though they had a spell of 1-4 without reply mid-game (helped by a James Naughton penalty after Micheál Aodh Martín fouled Robbie Childs; the referee black-carded the wrong Cork player). A nine-point loss. Full scorer list. Limerick's two-pointers (2: Naughton, Coleman); Cork hit none in this game.

scorers.note: defender, goal on the edge of the square

source.snippet: Scorers for Limerick: James Naughton 0-4 (2tpf), Barry Coleman 0-3 (1tp), Mark McCarthy 1-0, Eliah Riordan 0-2 (1tp), Rory O'Brien, Cillian Fahy 0-1 each.

results.note: An 11-point Tailteann opener defeat. Wexford led 0-12 to 1-3 at the break and put it away with two goals inside four minutes of the restart. Limerick had Peter Nash red-carded and Mark McCarthy sin-binned late. This Round 1 loss dropped Limerick into the Round 2B knockout. Full scorer list. Both sides hit three two-pointers.

source.snippet: Scorers for Limerick: Danny Neville 1-3 (1tp), James Naughton 1-2 (1-0p, 1f), Cillian Fahy 0-3 (1tp), Tony McCarthy 0-2, Barry Coleman 0-2 (1tp), Eliah O'Riordan 0-1, Peter Nash 0-1 (1f).

results.note: Elimination game. Limerick led 2-10 to 2-6 at half-time with the breeze, but Wicklow blitzed the third quarter (Dean Healy goal and two-pointer) and pulled away, the nine-point final margin inflated by a late Wicklow 1-3. With their first four midfield picks unavailable, Limerick were overrun for primary possession after the break. Full scorer list. Both sides hit four two-pointers. Ended Limerick's season.

collection_meta.collected_by: Claude (research session, not yet hand-verified by Rory)
collection_meta.notes: Limerick is a regression story: 2025 Tailteann Cup finalists who in 2026 leaked four goals to Cork in Munster and then went out of the Tailteann Cup at the Round 2B stage, undone by injuries and suspensions in midfield. James Naughton is the standout scorer across the campaign (penalties, two-point frees, frees). A clean three-game record with full scorer detail. Editorially a 'fall' county, paired naturally with Clare as Munster's two Tailteann under-performers.
